rationale: Contract-stability lens: PASS. 109 byte-compat identity holds. DefaultConfig panel values are value-unchanged (3+3, "n-1", claude_sub_on_quota=true, empty gates/substitutes/note); Count is pointerized but CountValue()==3. TestLoad_GatesAbsentByteIdentical109 is genuine (reflect.DeepEqual on zero-config + 109 AC7 fixture; every gate-scoped resolver returns global-derived over all PanelGateKeys for both; gates:{} == absent; count-less->1). PanelExpectedReviewers's empty-list path returns 6 by summing DefaultConfig, matching 109's literal `return 6`. Downstream surfaces are exactly as pinned: resolver signatures match spec R3/plan (all exported, fail-loud on unknown gate); PanelGateKeys order = R1 enum verbatim, declared once; CountValue pointerization is fully contained (only external reader migrated; report.go/reports.go/trace.go/budgeter.go .Count are unrelated structs; whole tree builds); ReviewerSlot{Slot,Model,Lens} maps 1:1 to R9 slots {slot,model,lens}. Verified: go build ./... exit 0, all six Bead-1 tests + TestConfigShow_EmitsPanelModelsLoop PASS, git status clean.

findings.summary: Empty-family reviewer entry: 109 accepted it (no family-value check, only count<1); 112 refuses via model()=="" (R4c). Narrowing at a degenerate edge, mildly against R2's universal monotonicity wording, but exactly what R4c requires — faithful to spec, and no real/documented 109 config (zero-config, AC7 fixture) hits it. Non-blocking observation.

ReviewerSlot has no JSON tags, so a direct marshal would emit {"Slot","Model","Lens"} not R9's {slot,model,lens}. Not a Bead-1 defect: the --gate --json serialization is Bead 3's scope (plan step 5 builds a typed json-tagged struct; TestConfigShowGate_ResolvedJSON pins the exact members). Field names map 1:1 so there is no rename risk. Flag for the Bead-3 reviewer, not this commit.

Positive: pointerizing Reviewer.Count is fully contained. Grep confirms cmd/mindspec/config.go was the sole external config.Reviewer.Count reader (migrated to CountValue()); report.go:172/236, reports.go:237, trace.go, budgeter.go .Count are all distinct structs. go build ./... exits 0 — no dangling %d-on-pointer or int-read survives.
